sourceMovie.title: To Live
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto To Live (1994) as a drama theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The drama and romance blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Curious Case of Benjamin Button
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If To Live clicked for you, The Curious Case of Benjamin Button (2008) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and romance, with emotional whiplash that feels honest. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after To Live?
faq.answer: Try The Curious Case of Benjamin Button, Indecent Proposal, Across the Universe—each shares DNA with To Live while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is To Live?
faq.answer: To Live (1994) is best described as Drama, Romance, War. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is To Live worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 8.1/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this drama lane.
